text:
REGIME — Broad lift faded at blue chips; small caps led, defensives held, discretionary lagged.

INDICES — VIX 23.9, holding elevated within 52-week range of 13.5–52.3. IWM +0.69%, QQQ +0.11%, SPY +0.09%, DIA -0.09%; small caps outperformed large caps cleanly. Most notable cross-asset move: USO surged +11.15% on the day.

SECTORS — XLRE +1.61%, XLK +0.80%, XLP +0.53%, XLU +0.50%, XLE +0.47%, XLC +0.41%, XLF +0.18%, XLB -0.10%, XLI -0.40%, XLV -0.62%, XLY -1.50%. Top industries: USO +11.15%, MSOS +4.62%; bottom: TAN -2.52%, SLV -3.45%.

THE 7s — Toll Road +1.64%, Semi +1.27%, Energy +0.91%, New Metals +0.87%, New Staples +0.83%, Cloud +0.75%, Defense +0.55%, Software +0.54%, Old Staples +0.50%, Insurance +0.32%, Buildout +0.19%, Freight +0.18%, Bank +0.15%, Power +0.04%, Medtech +0.00%, Old Metals -0.21%, Housing -0.71%, Health -0.76%, Industrial -0.92%, Fab 7 -1.21%. Strongest single ticker: watch Toll Road 7's top name; weakest in Fab 7.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 51.2, Neutral. Top movers: USO +11.15% (cross-asset), MSOS +4.62% (industries), SLV -3.45% (cross-asset). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +5.59%, worst XLY -7.02%; best basket Energy +8.04%, worst Software -8.35%. USO's +11.15% spike aligns with Brent at $121.88, up +0.41 on the day.

WATCH — IJT (vol spike 2.6x average, +0.53%): small-cap value divergence from IWM worth monitoring for follow-through or reversal. CPER (vol spike 2.2x, +0.09%): copper positioning stirring despite flat price; watch metals rotation signal. XLY (-1.50%, 20d -7.02%): consumer discretionary momentum breakdown accelerating — critical level if selling resumes Monday.
